name: Pearl Harbor Aviation Museum
tagline: Real WWII planes in original hangars on Ford Island — the flight simulators are a huge hit with older kids.
parking: Park at Pearl Harbor Visitor Center; shuttle bus to Ford Island included with admission
hours: Daily 9am–5pm (last entry 4pm). Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's
bestTime: Weekday mornings; pair with the free USS Arizona Memorial visit
talkStory: Best for ages 6 and up. Two original WWII hangars filled with restored planes you can get close to — the Japanese Zero and the B-25 bomber are jaw-droppers. The combat flight simulators ($) are the highlight for most kids. The museum tells the Pearl Harbor story in a way kids can grasp. Combine with the free USS Arizona Memorial and plan a full morning. No bags larger than a purse allowed at Pearl Harbor — leave them in the car.
